conbody.p: Overview: A Post-Discharge Review report helps a CDI manager to analyze the statistics around encounters that presents an opportunity to enhance reimbursement prior to final billing and post-discharge review activity.

The report provides answers to the following questions.
How many encounters were presented for review before final billing?
How many post-discharge reviews were performed?
What percentage of presented cases were reviewed by the reviewer?
What percentage of presented cases were for a specific opportunity? What percentage of presented cases were for PDX sequencing opportunities?
What percentage of presented cases were resolved by identifying reimbursable secondary diagnosis?
What are the details around the encounters in which the DRG/WT arrived at by the Coder (Final coded) is different from the final billed DRG, DRG WT?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Post-Discharge Reviews Post-Discharge Reviews Overview .

Post-Discharge Reviews Overview report displays the following:
KPI Cards: Encounters with Impact Opportunity, Encounters Reviewed, Encounters Impacted, Overall Reimbursement Impact
Impact Opportunities Identified and Addressed Chart
Post-Discharge Review Details Tabular Chart
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters on all pages, refer .

entry.p: The count of encounters with at-least one post-discharge review opportunity identified.
This KPI displays the count of encounters presented to the Post-Discharge Review worklist.
The count and percentage of encounters reviewed by the post-discharge case reviewer.

ul.li: Format: Count of Encounters Reviewed (Percentage of Encounters with Impact Opportunity reviewed)
Count: Out of total 'Encounters with Impact Opportunity', the count of encounters with at-least one resolved opportunity or 'No Further Action Required' checked.

Percentage: (Encounters Reviewed/ Encounters with Impact Opportunity) x 100) = (Count of encounters with at-least one resolved opportunity / Count of encounters with at-least one post-discharge review opportunity identified) x 100) Rounded to 0 decimal places. E.g. 10%

entry.p: The count and percentage of reviewed encounters for which the post-discharge review resulted in a higher reimbursement.
Out of Encounters Reviewed, the count of encounters in which the Final Billed DRG WT was greater than the Final Coded DRG WT.

ul.li: Format: Count of Encounters Impacted (Percentage of Encounters with Impact Opportunity impacted)
Count: Out of total 'Encounters with Impact Opportunity', the count of encounters with Final Billed DRG WT greater than the Final Coded DRG WT.

Percentage: (Encounters Impacted / Encounters with Impact Opportunity) x 100) = ( Count of encounters with Final Billed DRG WT greater than the Final Coded DRG WT / Count of encounters with at-least one post-discharge review opportunity identified) x 100) Rounded to 0 decimal places. Example: 10%

entry.p: The total expected reimbursement resulting from shift in the weight between Final Billed DRG and Final Coded DRG weights for Reconciled Encounters (Auto- Reconciled, Reconciled-Impact or Reconciled-No Impact) in which Final Billed WT >= Final Coded WT (Final Billed WT - Final Coded WT >= 0)

Overall Reimbursement Impact Post-Discharge = (The sum of Reimbursement Impact ($) of all Reconciled Encounters (Auto- Reconciled, Reconciled-Impact or Reconciled-No Impact) with positive Overall WT Shift) * Blended Rate

p.note: Total Reimbursement Impact ($) rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Encounters with negative WT shift i.e. (Final Billed WT - Final Coded WT < 0) are not considered in this metric calculation.
Example: $ 5,234.00

p.b: Post-Discharge Reviews Overview - Impact Opportunities Identified and Addressed Chart

fig.title: Impact Opportunities Identified and Addressed Chart

ul.li: The ‘Impact Opportunities Identified and Addressed ’ chart displays the count of opportunities identified and addressed by the reviewer for each impact opportunity type.
X-axis: Displays the description of the Opportunities Identified Example: Resequencing PDx
Y-axis: Displays the count of opportunities.
Sort: Chart is sorted by the opportunity type with the highest identified opportunities in descending order.

p.b: Post-Discharge Reviews Overview - Post-Discharge Review Details Tabular Chart

conbody.p: This chart displays the details of the encounters presented to the Post-Discharge review worklist (i.e. list of encounters with at-least one post-discharge review opportunity identified).

ul.li: Format: Tabular
One row in the table corresponds to one encounter.

row.entry:
The facility assigned to the encounter.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
The Account Number assigned to the encounter.
The MRN assigned to the encounter.

entry.p: The Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
The Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.

row.entry: 'Y' gets displayed for the encounter with at-least one resolved opportunity or 'No Further Action Required' checked.

entry.p: The last date and time (Facility Timezone) on which the reviewer reviewed the case.

p.note: Last Date/Time is captured when you click the 'Done' button on the Post-Discharge Review Checklist pop-up.

entry.p: The name of the post-discharge case reviewer.
Format : Last Name, First Name (Reviewer ID)
Reviewer is a user that has the Post-Discharge Reviewer or Post-Discharge Manager role assigned.
The impact opportunities identified for the encounter in a comma-separated format.
Example: Resequencing PDx, PSI/HAC Status Review.
The recommendation made by the reviewer in a comma-separated format.
Example: Resequencing PDx, Resolved Clarification, Reimbursable Secondary Diagnosis.
‘Y’ gets displayed when the reviewer has selected ‘No Further Action Required’ check box on the Post-Discharge Review Checklist screen.

p.note: Not all the reviewed cases have 'No Further Action Required' checked.

entry.p: The code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393
The code and description of the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393
The code and description of the Final Billed DRG.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the Final Billed DRG.
Example: 1.0393
Final Billed DRG WT > Final Coded DRG WT (Final Billed DRG WT is displayed in green font.)
Final Billed DRG WT < Final Coded DRG WT (Final Billed DRG WT is displayed in orange font.)
Final Billed DRG WT = Final Coded DRG WT (Final Billed DRG WT is displayed in normal font.)

row.entry: Shift in the weight between Final Billed DRG WT and Final Coded DRG WT.

ul.li: Variance = Final Billed DRG WT - Final Coded DRG WT

Rounded to 4 decimal places.
Example: 1.0178
Negative ‘Variance’ is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: The Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
The ‘Post-Discharge Review Reimbursement Impact ’ value for the encounter.
'Reimbursement Impact' for an encounter is calculated using the below formula:

ul.li: Reimbursement Impact = Variance x Blended Rate

Rounded to 0 decimal places.
Example: $ 5,234

Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact’ is displayed in the red font.
Example: $ 5,234

entry.p: The Encounter Status assigned to the encounter.
Example: Reconciled No Impact
The Payer assigned to the encounter.
Example: Medicare (MED)
The Unit assigned to the encounter.
Example: PODA

ul.li: Displays the discharge date range.
You can select date from the lookup provided.
Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.
With the Reset button, you can reset the discharge date range.
Tooltip: Use "Cltr + Click" to reset the Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL
Displays the list of Opportunity Types. This list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple opportunities.

Format: Opportunity Description
Example: Resequencing PDx, Unresolved Clarification, PSI/HAC Status review, etc.
Default: ALL
Displays a list which includes Y, N, ALL.
Y: Displays data for the encounters for which the reviewer selected ‘No Further Action Required’ checkbox on the Post-Discharge Review Checklist screen.
N: Displays data for the encounters with ‘No Further Action Required’ checkbox unchecked on the Post-Discharge Review Checklist screen.
Default: ALL

entry.p: Displays the list of active and inactive users with the Post-Discharge Reviewer or Post-Discharge Manager role assigned.
Format: Last Name, First Name (User ID)

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL

row.entry: The count of encounters with at-least one post-discharge review opportunity identified.

entry.p: Encounters Reviewed: The count of encounters reviewed by the post-discharge case reviewer.
%Reviewed = (Encounters Reviewed by the post-discharge case reviewer/ Encounters with Impact Opportunity) x 100). E.g. 10%
Encounters Impacted: The count reviewed encounters for which the post-discharge review resulted in a higher reimbursement.
%Impacted = (Encounters Impacted / Encounters with Impact Opportunity) x 100) E.g. 10%

row.entry: Identified: Count of potential post review impact opportunities identified for the encounter. E.g. Resequencing PDx, PSI/HAC Status Review.
Addressed: Count of post review impact opportunities addressed/reviewed by the post-discharge case reviewer.

entry.p: Post-Discharge Review Reimbursement Impact = (Variance x Blended Rate) E.g. $ 15,234
Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact’ is displayed in red font.
